message:
feat(robot): add --robot-health-oauth flag for OAuth/rate-limit status (bd-2plo3)

Add new robot mode command to get per-agent OAuth and rate-limit status:
- GetHealthOAuth() returns per-agent OAuth status (valid/expired/error)
- Per-agent rate-limit status (ok/warning/limited) with cooldown tracking
- Integration with BackoffManager for accurate cooldown times
- New dashboard panel (RateLimitPanel) for TUI display
- Unit tests for OAuth detection and rate-limit parsing

Usage: ntm --robot-health-oauth=SESSION

Returns JSON with:
- agents[]: per-agent OAuth/rate-limit status
- summary: aggregate counts for OK/warning/limited states

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2pb6e","title":"Task: Implement mode-to-agent assignment strategies in internal/ensemble/assignment.go","description":"# Task: Implement Mode-to-Agent Assignment Strategies

## Location
internal/ensemble/assignment.go

## Purpose
Assign reasoning modes to agent panes while respecting agent strengths, user preferences, and available pane types.

## Assignment Strategies

### 1. Round-Robin (default)
Distribute modes evenly across available panes.
```go
func AssignRoundRobin(modes []string, panes []tmux.Pane) []ModeAssignment
```

### 2. Category-Affinity (intelligent)
Prefer agent types based on **ModeCategory** (A–L taxonomy), not hard-coded mode IDs.
```go
// Category -> preferred agent types (ordered)
var CategoryAffinities = map[ModeCategory][]string{
    CategoryFormal:      {"cc", "cod"},
    CategoryUncertainty: {"cod", "cc"},
    CategoryCausal:      {"cc", "cod"},
    CategoryPractical:   {"cod", "cc"},
    CategoryStrategic:   {"cc", "cod"},
    CategoryDialectical: {"cc"},
    CategoryModal:       {"cc", "cod"},
    CategoryDomain:      {"cc", "gmi"},
    CategoryMeta:        {"cc"},
    CategoryAmpliative:  {"gmi", "cc"},
    CategoryVagueness:   {"gmi", "cc"},
    CategoryChange:      {"cc", "cod"},
}

func AssignByCategory(modes []string, panes []tmux.Pane, catalog *ModeCatalog) []ModeAssignment
```
- Resolve mode by **id or code**
- Pick available pane of preferred type; fallback to any

### 3. Explicit (user-specified)
User provides exact mapping: `abductive:cc,adversarial:cod` or `A1:cc`.
```go
func AssignExplicit(specs []string, panes []tmux.Pane) ([]ModeAssignment, error)
```

## Helper Functions
```go
func groupPanesByType(panes []tmux.Pane) map[string][]tmux.Pane
func pickAvailablePane(byType map[string][]tmux.Pane, preferred []string, used []ModeAssignment) tmux.Pane
func ValidateAssignments(assignments []ModeAssignment, modes []string) error
```

## Determinism + Logging
- Assignment order must be deterministic (stable ordering by mode id/code).
- Tie-breaking is stable and logged (preferred type chosen + fallback reason).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Round-robin distributes evenly
- [ ] Category-affinity uses taxonomy categories (A–L) only
- [ ] Explicit assignment parses id or code
- [ ] Handles more modes than panes (error)
- [ ] Handles more panes than modes (some panes unused)
- [ ] Validation catches invalid configurations
- [ ] Logs assignment decisions + fallbacks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:36:16.389957944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T22:43:51.069750128Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T22:43:51.069729149Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pb6e","depends_on_id":"bd-2qfa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2pgo6","title":"CLI: Add tier threshold override flags","description":"## Purpose
Implement --prompt flag to provide initial marching orders that will be injected into all spawned agents after launch.

## Implementation Details

```go
// Flag definition
var initialPrompt string
var promptFile   string

func init() {
    swarmCmd.Flags().StringVar(&initialPrompt, "prompt", "",
        "Initial prompt to inject into all agents")
    swarmCmd.Flags().StringVar(&promptFile, "prompt-file", "",
        "File containing initial prompt (mutually exclusive with --prompt)")
}

// Validation
func runSwarm(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    logger := slog.Default()
    
    // Mutual exclusivity check
    if initialPrompt != "" && promptFile != "" {
        return fmt.Errorf("--prompt and --prompt-file are mutually exclusive")
    }
    
    // Load prompt from file if specified
    if promptFile != "" {
        data, err := os.ReadFile(promptFile)
        if err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("read prompt file %q: %w", promptFile, err)
        }
        initialPrompt = string(data)
        logger.Info("loaded prompt from file",
            "path", promptFile,
            "length", len(initialPrompt))
    }
    
    if initialPrompt != "" {
        logger.Info("initial prompt configured",
            "length", len(initialPrompt),
            "preview", truncate(initialPrompt, 50))
    } else {
        logger.Debug("no initial prompt configured")
    }
    
    // Store in plan for injection phase
    plan.InitialPrompt = initialPrompt
    
    // ... continue ...
}

// After agent launch, inject prompt
func injectInitialPrompts(plan *swarm.SwarmPlan) error {
    if plan.InitialPrompt == "" {
        return nil
    }
    
    injector := swarm.NewPromptInjector(logger)
    for _, session := range plan.Sessions {
        for _, pane := range session.Panes {
            if err := injector.Inject(pane, plan.InitialPrompt); err != nil {
                logger.Error("failed to inject prompt",
                    "pane", pane.Index,
                    "error", err)
            }
        }
    }
    return nil
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log prompt length (not full content for large prompts)
- Log first 50 chars as preview
- Log prompt source (flag vs file)
- Log each injection attempt and result

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --prompt for inline text
- [ ] --prompt-file for file input
- [ ] Mutual exclusivity enforced
- [ ] File read error handling
- [ ] Preview logging (truncated)
- [ ] Injection after all agents launched
- [ ] Empty prompt means no injection","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:22.621959652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T16:51:36.909307297Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pgo6","depends_on_id":"bd-3ixg4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pgo6","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2pl9v","title":"Task: Write preamble content for all modes (core files + advanced fallback)","description":"# Task: Write preamble content for all modes (core files + advanced fallback)

Each mode preamble explains the reasoning lens without exposing implementation details to users.

## Strategy
- **Core tier (~28):** hand-authored preamble files.
- **Advanced tier (remaining modes):** auto-generated fallback preamble derived from mode metadata (description, outputs, best_for, failure_modes). This ensures **all 80 modes are usable** without requiring bespoke files.

## Preamble File Format (Core Modes)
```yaml
# internal/ensemble/preambles/deductive.yaml
id: deductive
code: A1
name: Deductive Inference
tier: core
preamble: |
  ## Your Reasoning Mode: Deductive Inference
  ...
```

## File Location
internal/ensemble/preambles/*.yaml

## Fallback Behavior (Advanced Modes)
- If no file exists for a mode, PreambleEngine renders:
  - Mode name + description
  - Outputs, best_for, failure_modes
  - Differentiator (if present)
  - Tier warning ("advanced" mode)

## Validation / Tests
- Preambles must pass bd-xkrnc quality validation (required sections, schema contract fields, token budget)
- No internal implementation details or private instructions in preamble text

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All **core** modes have preamble files
- [ ] **All 80 modes** can render a preamble (file or fallback)
- [ ] Preamble includes id, code, name, tier
- [ ] Content matches `modes_of_reasoning.md` for core modes
- [ ] Fallback preamble uses mode metadata deterministically","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:33:36.480494155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T15:45:09.268301537Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T15:45:09.268260931Z","close_reason":"Added core preamble YAML files for all TierCore modes; fallback remains metadata-driven.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pl9v","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pl9v","depends_on_id":"bd-1hlhd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pl9v","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2plo3","title":"Session health dashboard should show OAuth/rate-limit status per agent","description":"Session health dashboard should show OAuth/rate-limit status per agent.

## Problem Being Solved

When running multi-agent swarms, agents frequently hit rate limits or OAuth issues. Currently there's no visibility into which agents are affected and why.

## Requirements

### Dashboard Widget
Add a new widget to the session health dashboard showing:
1. Per-agent OAuth status (valid/expired/error)
2. Per-agent rate limit status (ok/warning/limited)
3. Last API call timestamp
4. Error count in last N minutes

### Data Sources
- OAuth status: detect from agent output patterns
- Rate limit status: integrate with ratelimit.Tracker
- Timestamps: from pane capture output

### Visual Design
```
OAuth/Rate Status:
  cc-1: OAuth=✓ Rate=OK   Last=2m ago
  cc-2: OAuth=✓ Rate=WARN Last=30s ago (3 limits in 5m)
  cod-1: OAuth=✓ Rate=OK  Last=1m ago
  gmi-1: OAuth=\! Rate=LIMITED Last=5m ago (needs reauth)
```

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-health-oauth=SESSION
Returns: JSON with per-agent OAuth and rate limit status

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Dashboard widget shows OAuth status per agent (valid/expired/error)
2. Dashboard widget shows rate limit status per agent (ok/warning/limited)
3. Warning status triggered at 3+ rate limits in 5 minutes
4. Limited status triggered when agent reports rate limit error
5. Robot mode command returns machine-readable status
6. Status updates within 30 seconds of state change

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/dashboard/oauth_widget_test.go
- Test status detection from output patterns
- Test warning/limited threshold logic

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-OAUTH-DASH] OAuth Dashboard Widget Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-OAUTH-DASH]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing OAuth dashboard widget"
TEST_SESSION="e2e_oauth_$$"
ntm spawn "$TEST_SESSION" --cc=1 --no-prompt
sleep 5
# Check robot health command exists
ntm --robot-health-oauth="$TEST_SESSION" | jq -e '.agents' || echo "WARN: command not yet implemented"
ntm kill "$TEST_SESSION" --force
echo "$LOG_PREFIX OAuth dashboard test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Session health dashboard shows OAuth status per agent
2. Rate limit status visible for each provider
3. Color coding for healthy/warning/critical
4. Real-time updates from rate limit tracker
5. Unit tests for status rendering
6. E2E test verifying dashboard accuracy","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-20T05:28:28.060979675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:14:25.106423672Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-2pwzf","title":"Task: --robot-agent-health Command Implementation","description":"## Task: --robot-agent-health Command Implementation

### Why This Command Exists

While `--robot-is-working` answers "is this agent busy?", `--robot-agent-health` provides the FULL health picture by combining:

1. **Local state** - Parsed from pane output (via parser module)
2. **Provider usage** - Account-level limits from caut integration
3. **Health score** - Computed metric for decision-making

This enables sophisticated controller decisions like:
- "Agent is idle but account is at 90% - wait before sending more work"
- "Agent looks idle but we could not determine provider usage - proceed with caution"

### Command Specification

```bash
# Full health check (includes caut query)
ntm --robot-agent-health=myproject --panes=2

# Check multiple panes
ntm --robot-agent-health=myproject --panes=2,3,4

# Skip caut query (faster, local-only)
ntm --robot-agent-health=myproject --panes=2 --no-caut

# Include raw samples for debugging
ntm --robot-agent-health=myproject --panes=all --verbose
```

### Output Format (JSON)

```json

```

### Health Score Calculation (0-100)

```go
func calculateHealthScore(localState *agent.AgentState, providerUsage *caut.ProviderPayload) int {
    score := 100
    
    // Local state deductions
    if localState.IsRateLimited {
        score -= 50  // Critical - agent blocked
    }
    if localState.IsInError {
        score -= 40  // Serious - needs attention
    }
    if localState.IsContextLow && !localState.IsWorking {
        score -= 25  // Should restart soon
    }
    if localState.IsContextLow && localState.IsWorking {
        score -= 10  // Working but limited runway
    }
    if localState.Type == agent.AgentTypeUnknown {
        score -= 15  // Uncertainty penalty
    }
    
    // Provider usage deductions (if available)
    if providerUsage != nil {
        if pct := providerUsage.UsedPercent(); pct != nil {
            if *pct >= 95 {
                score -= 30  // Near cap
            } else if *pct >= 80 {
                score -= 15  // Approaching limit
            } else if *pct >= 60 {
                score -= 5   // Moderate usage
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Confidence adjustment
    if localState.Confidence < 0.5 {
        score -= 10  // Low confidence in assessment
    }
    
    // Floor at 0
    if score < 0 {
        score = 0
    }
    
    return score
}

func healthGrade(score int) string {
    switch {
    case score >= 90:
        return "A"
    case score >= 80:
        return "B"
    case score >= 70:
        return "C"
    case score >= 50:
        return "D"
    default:
        return "F"
    }
}
```

### Health Recommendations

| Recommendation | Trigger | Controller Action |
|----------------|---------|-------------------|
| `HEALTHY` | Score >= 70, no issues | Continue normally |
| `MONITOR` | Score 50-70, minor issues | Keep watching |
| `RESTART_RECOMMENDED` | Idle + low context | Restart when convenient |
| `RESTART_URGENT` | Error state or stuck | Restart immediately |
| `WAIT_FOR_RESET` | Rate limited | Wait for reset time |
| `SWITCH_ACCOUNT` | Provider at 90%+ | Consider caam account switch |

### Implementation Structure

```go
package robot

type AgentHealthResult struct {
    Session       string                       `json:"session"`
    Timestamp     string                       `json:"timestamp"`
    CautAvailable bool                         `json:"caut_available"`
    Panes         map[string]PaneHealthStatus  `json:"panes"`
    ProviderSummary map[string]ProviderStats   `json:"provider_summary"`
    FleetHealth   FleetHealthSummary           `json:"fleet_health"`
}

type PaneHealthStatus struct {
    AgentType            string              `json:"agent_type"`
    LocalState           LocalStateInfo      `json:"local_state"`
    ProviderUsage        *ProviderUsageInfo  `json:"provider_usage,omitempty"`
    HealthScore          int                 `json:"health_score"`
    HealthGrade          string              `json:"health_grade"`
    Issues               []string            `json:"issues"`
    Recommendation       string              `json:"recommendation"`
    RecommendationReason string              `json:"recommendation_reason"`
}

func AgentHealth(session string, panes []int, includeCaut bool) (*AgentHealthResult, error) {
    result := &AgentHealthResult{
        Session:       session,
        Timestamp:     time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
        Panes:         make(map[string]PaneHealthStatus),
        ProviderSummary: make(map[string]ProviderStats),
    }
    
    // Step 1: Get local state for all panes (--robot-is-working internally)
    isWorkingResult, err := IsWorking(session, panes, false)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get working status: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Step 2: Query caut for provider usage (if enabled)
    var cautClient *caut.CachedClient
    if includeCaut {
        cautClient = caut.NewCachedClient(time.Minute * 5)
        result.CautAvailable = cautClient.IsAvailable()
    }
    
    // Step 3: Build health status for each pane
    for paneStr, workStatus := range isWorkingResult.Panes {
        healthStatus := PaneHealthStatus{
            AgentType: workStatus.AgentType,
            LocalState: LocalStateInfo{
                IsWorking:        workStatus.IsWorking,
                IsIdle:           workStatus.IsIdle,
                IsRateLimited:    workStatus.IsRateLimited,
                IsContextLow:     workStatus.IsContextLow,
                ContextRemaining: workStatus.ContextRemaining,
                Confidence:       workStatus.Confidence,
                Indicators:       workStatus.Indicators,
            },
        }
        
        // Query provider usage if caut available
        if cautClient != nil && result.CautAvailable {
            provider := caut.AgentTypeToProvider(workStatus.AgentType)
            if provider != "" {
                if usage, err := cautClient.GetProviderUsage(context.Background(), provider); err == nil {
                    healthStatus.ProviderUsage = convertProviderUsage(usage)
                    
                    // Track in provider summary
                    paneNum, _ := strconv.Atoi(paneStr)
                    updateProviderSummary(result.ProviderSummary, provider, usage, paneNum)
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Calculate health score and recommendation
        healthStatus.HealthScore = calculateHealthScore(&workStatus, healthStatus.ProviderUsage)
        healthStatus.HealthGrade = healthGrade(healthStatus.HealthScore)
        healthStatus.Issues = collectIssues(&workStatus, healthStatus.ProviderUsage)
        healthStatus.Recommendation, healthStatus.RecommendationReason = 
            deriveHealthRecommendation(&workStatus, healthStatus.ProviderUsage, healthStatus.HealthScore)
        
        result.Panes[paneStr] = healthStatus
    }
    
    // Step 4: Calculate fleet health summary
    result.FleetHealth = calculateFleetHealth(result.Panes)
    
    return result, nil
}
```

### Files to Create/Modify

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `cmd/ntm/robot_agent_health.go` | CLI flag handling |
| `internal/robot/agent_health.go` | Core logic |
| `internal/robot/health_score.go` | Scoring algorithms |
| `internal/robot/agent_health_test.go` | Unit tests |

### Why This Combines Local + Provider Data

Consider this scenario:

- **Local state**: Agent appears idle, no recent output
- **Provider usage**: Account at 95% usage

A naive controller might restart this agent. But with provider context, we know:
- The restart will likely hit the same rate limit
- Better to wait for reset or switch accounts first

This is why `--robot-agent-health` exists - to provide the FULL picture for intelligent decisions.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Combines local state from parser + caut provider data
- [ ] Health score accurately reflects agent wellbeing
- [ ] Works without caut (--no-caut flag, graceful degradation)
- [ ] Provider summary aggregates across panes
- [ ] Fleet health gives overall session view
- [ ] Issues list is actionable
- [ ] Recommendations are clear and useful
- [ ] Unit tests cover various health scenarios","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-20T21:37:59.947178119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T02:44:30.058639559Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T02:44:30.058556332Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-agent-health command. Created health_score.go (scoring algorithms), agent_health.go (core logic combining local state + caut provider data), agent_health_test.go (29+ tests). Added CLI flags: --robot-agent-health, --no-caut, --agent-health-verbose. All tests pass, build compiles.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pwzf","depends_on_id":"bd-16ptx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pwzf","depends_on_id":"bd-1bdxp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pwzf","depends_on_id":"bd-2iw6e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pwzf","depends_on_id":"bd-3uctb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3didt","title":"Feature: Partial Synthesis Recovery","description":"## Summary
Enable recovery from partial ensemble failures by checkpointing mode outputs and allowing resume/rerun.

## Implementation
- Checkpoint after each completed mode (atomic write) with metadata: run_id, mode_id, context_hash, status.
- `ntm ensemble resume <run-id>` continues from last checkpoint.
- `ntm ensemble rerun-mode <run-id> <mode>` retries a specific failed mode.
- Cleanup checkpoints on successful completion; provide manual cleanup command.

## Logging
- Log checkpoint write/read events, resume actions, rerun targets.

## Tests
- Unit: checkpoint save/restore + integrity checks
- Integration: resume skips completed modes
- E2E: failure → resume → success flow

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checkpoints created after each mode
- [ ] Resume restores state correctly
- [ ] Rerun single mode works
- [ ] Cleanup occurs after success
- [ ] Logs show checkpoint lifecycle

## Dependencies
- EnsembleManager (bd-lp5h4)
- State persistence (bd-25wul)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:08:18.915853635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T03:07:49.783690679Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T03:07:49.783423907Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3didt","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3didt","depends_on_id":"bd-25wul","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3didt","depends_on_id":"bd-lp5h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3dqbk","title":"E2E Tests: Robot Restart Pane","description":"E2E tests for Robot Restart Pane with actual tmux sessions.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Soft Exit Success
Spawn session with claude agent, verify running, call --robot-restart-pane, verify exit_method='soft', new agent running

### Scenario 2: Hard Kill Required
Spawn session, make agent unresponsive, call --robot-restart-pane, verify exit_method='kill', new agent running

### Scenario 3: With Bead Assignment
Spawn session, create test bead, call --robot-restart-pane --bead=X, verify bead_assigned, prompt in pane output

### Scenario 4: Invalid Pane
Call with non-existent pane, verify error response with available panes

### Scenario 5: Timing Verification
Instrument timing in restart sequence, verify 100ms gap between Ctrl-Cs, total time reasonable

## Test Infrastructure

1. All 5 test scenarios implemented as bash scripts
2. Soft exit verified by checking agent gracefully terminates
3. Hard kill verified by checking agent forcibly terminated
4. Bead assignment verified by checking pane output contains prompt
5. Timing verification confirms 100ms gaps
6. Cleanup removes all test sessions

 {"id":"bd-3ef1l","title":"Task: WebSocket streaming tests","description":"## Scope\nAutomated tests for WebSocket streaming, resume, and backpressure.\n\n## Logging Requirements\n- Log subscription topics, seq numbers, and dropped counts.\n- Log reconnect attempts and cursor resets.\n\n## Tests\n- Subscribe to pane output and verify sequence continuity.\n- Disconnect/reconnect with since cursor.\n- Force backpressure and verify pane.output.dropped event.\n- Multi‑client fan‑out correctness.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- WS tests pass deterministically.\n- Resume and backpressure behaviors validated.\n","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:55:08.244291999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T00:09:09.996911230Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ef1l","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2wn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ef1l","depends_on_id":"bd-cs5x4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ef1l","depends_on_id":"bd-excs1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ejl","title":"Add ntm send --base-prompt flag - prepend common instructions to all prompts automatically","description":"## Overview
Add --base-prompt flag to prepend common instructions to all prompts automatically.

## Problem
Users often want to include standard instructions (coding standards, project context, safety rules) with every prompt but don't want to repeat them.

## Solution
`ntm send myproject --base-prompt "Always run tests after changes" --msg "Implement feature X"`

The actual prompt sent becomes:
```
Always run tests after changes

Implement feature X
```

## Configuration
Can also be set in ntm.yaml:
```yaml
send:
  base_prompt: |
    Follow AGENTS.md rules.
    Run ubs before committing.
    Use br to track work.
```

## Implementation
```go
func buildPrompt(basePrompt, userPrompt string) string {
    if basePrompt == "" {
        return userPrompt
    }
    return basePrompt + "\n\n" + userPrompt
}
```

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Inline base prompt
ntm send myproject --base-prompt "Run tests" --msg "Add feature"

# From file
ntm send myproject --base-prompt-file context.md --msg "Add feature"

# With batch (prepended to each)
ntm send myproject --base-prompt "Be careful" --batch prompts.txt
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Base prompt prepended to all messages
- [ ] Works with --msg and --batch
- [ ] Config file support
- [ ] File-based base prompt (--base-prompt-file)
- [ ] Empty base prompt = no-op
- [ ] Clear separator between base and user prompt

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Prompt concatenation
- Unit test: Config file loading
- E2E test: Base prompt with batch send","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 }
+
+// =============================================================================
+// OAuth and Rate Limit Status API (bd-2plo3)
+// =============================================================================
+
+// OAuthStatus represents OAuth authentication status for an agent
+type OAuthStatus string
+
+const (
+	OAuthValid   OAuthStatus = "valid"
+	OAuthExpired OAuthStatus = "expired"
+	OAuthError   OAuthStatus = "error"
+	OAuthUnknown OAuthStatus = "unknown"
+)
+
+// RateLimitStatus represents rate limit status for an agent
+type RateLimitStatus string
+
+const (
+	RateLimitOK      RateLimitStatus = "ok"
+	RateLimitWarning RateLimitStatus = "warning" // 3+ limits in 5 minutes
+	RateLimitLimited RateLimitStatus = "limited"
+)
+
+// AgentOAuthHealth contains OAuth and rate limit status for a single agent
+type AgentOAuthHealth struct {
+	Pane              int             `json:"pane"`
+	AgentType         string          `json:"agent_type"`
+	Provider          string          `json:"provider"` // anthropic, openai, google
+	OAuthStatus       OAuthStatus     `json:"oauth_status"`
+	OAuthError        string          `json:"oauth_error,omitempty"`
+	RateLimitStatus   RateLimitStatus `json:"rate_limit_status"`
+	RateLimitCount    int             `json:"rate_limit_count"`    // limits in window
+	CooldownRemaining int             `json:"cooldown_remaining"`  // seconds
+	LastActivitySec   int             `json:"last_activity_sec"`
+	ErrorCount        int             `json:"error_count"` // errors in last 5 minutes
+}
+
+// OAuthHealthOutput is the response format for --robot-health-oauth=SESSION
+type OAuthHealthOutput struct {
+	RobotResponse
+	Session   string             `json:"session"`
+	CheckedAt time.Time          `json:"checked_at"`
+	Agents    []AgentOAuthHealth `json:"agents"`
+	Summary   OAuthHealthSummary `json:"summary"`
+}
+
+// OAuthHealthSummary contains aggregate OAuth/rate limit status
+type OAuthHealthSummary struct {
+	Total         int `json:"total"`
+	OAuthValid    int `json:"oauth_valid"`
+	OAuthExpired  int `json:"oauth_expired"`
+	OAuthError    int `json:"oauth_error"`
+	RateLimitOK   int `json:"rate_limit_ok"`
+	RateLimitWarn int `json:"rate_limit_warn"`
+	RateLimited   int `json:"rate_limited"`
+}
+
+// GetHealthOAuth collects per-agent OAuth and rate limit status for a session.
+// This function returns the data struct directly, enabling CLI/REST parity.
+func GetHealthOAuth(session string) (*OAuthHealthOutput, error) {
+	output := &OAuthHealthOutput{
+		RobotResponse: NewRobotResponse(true),
+		Session:       session,
+		CheckedAt:     time.Now().UTC(),
+		Agents:        []AgentOAuthHealth{},
+		Summary:       OAuthHealthSummary{},
+	}
+
+	// Check if session exists
+	if !tmux.SessionExists(session) {
+		output.RobotResponse = NewErrorResponse(
+			fmt.Errorf("session '%s' not found", session),
+			ErrCodeSessionNotFound,
+			"Use --robot-status to list available sessions",
+		)
+		return output, nil
+	}
+
+	// Get panes in the session
+	panes, err := tmux.GetPanes(session)
+	if err != nil {
+		output.RobotResponse = NewErrorResponse(
+			err,
+			ErrCodeInternalError,
+			"Check tmux session state",
+		)
+		return output, nil
+	}
+
+	// Check OAuth/rate limit status for each agent pane
+	for _, pane := range panes {
+		agentType := detectAgentTypeFromPane(pane)
+		if agentType == "user" || agentType == "unknown" {
+			continue // Skip non-agent panes
+		}
+
+		agentHealth := getAgentOAuthHealth(session, pane, agentType)
+		output.Agents = append(output.Agents, agentHealth)
+
+		// Update summary
+		output.Summary.Total++
+		switch agentHealth.OAuthStatus {
+		case OAuthValid:
+			output.Summary.OAuthValid++
+		case OAuthExpired:
+			output.Summary.OAuthExpired++
+		case OAuthError:
+			output.Summary.OAuthError++
+		}
+		switch agentHealth.RateLimitStatus {
+		case RateLimitOK:
+			output.Summary.RateLimitOK++
+		case RateLimitWarning:
+			output.Summary.RateLimitWarn++
+		case RateLimitLimited:
+			output.Summary.RateLimited++
+		}
+	}
+
+	return output, nil
+}
+
+// getAgentOAuthHealth determines OAuth and rate limit status for a single agent
+func getAgentOAuthHealth(session string, pane tmux.Pane, agentType string) AgentOAuthHealth {
+	health := AgentOAuthHealth{
+		Pane:            pane.Index,
+		AgentType:       agentType,
+		Provider:        agentTypeToProvider(agentType),
+		OAuthStatus:     OAuthUnknown,
+		RateLimitStatus: RateLimitOK,
+	}
+
+	// Get activity time
+	activityTime, err := tmux.GetPaneActivity(pane.ID)
+	if err == nil {
+		health.LastActivitySec = int(time.Since(activityTime).Seconds())
+	}
+
+	// Capture recent output for OAuth/error detection
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 2*time.Second)
+	defer cancel()
+	output, err := tmux.CapturePaneOutputContext(ctx, pane.ID, 50)
+	if err != nil {
+		return health
+	}
+
+	output = stripANSI(output)
+	outputLower := strings.ToLower(output)
+
+	// Detect OAuth status from output patterns
+	health.OAuthStatus, health.OAuthError = detectOAuthStatus(outputLower)
+
+	// Detect rate limit status
+	health.RateLimitStatus, health.RateLimitCount = detectRateLimitStatusFromOutput(outputLower)
+
+	// Check cooldown from backoff manager
+	manager := GetBackoffManager(session)
+	remaining := manager.GetBackoffRemaining(pane.ID)
+	if remaining > 0 {
+		health.CooldownRemaining = int(remaining.Seconds())
+		health.RateLimitStatus = RateLimitLimited
+	}
+
+	// Count rate limits from backoff manager if we have data
+	if backoff := manager.GetBackoff(pane.ID); backoff != nil {
+		if backoff.TotalRateLimits > health.RateLimitCount {
+			health.RateLimitCount = backoff.TotalRateLimits
+		}
+	}
+
+	// Count errors in output
+	health.ErrorCount = countErrorsInOutput(outputLower)
+
+	// If rate limit count >= 3, upgrade to warning
+	if health.RateLimitStatus == RateLimitOK && health.RateLimitCount >= 3 {
+		health.RateLimitStatus = RateLimitWarning
+	}
+
+	return health
+}
+
+// agentTypeToProvider maps agent type to provider name
+func agentTypeToProvider(agentType string) string {
+	switch agentType {
+	case "claude", "cc":
+		return "anthropic"
+	case "codex", "cod":
+		return "openai"
+	case "gemini", "gmi":
+		return "google"
+	default:
+		return "unknown"
+	}
+}
+
+// detectOAuthStatus detects OAuth status from pane output
+func detectOAuthStatus(outputLower string) (OAuthStatus, string) {
+	// Check for explicit authentication errors
+	authErrorPatterns := []struct {
+		pattern string
+		status  OAuthStatus
+		message string
+	}{
+		{"authentication failed", OAuthError, "authentication failed"},
+		{"authentication error", OAuthError, "authentication error"},
+		{"invalid api key", OAuthError, "invalid API key"},
+		{"api key not found", OAuthError, "API key not found"},
+		{"unauthorized", OAuthError, "unauthorized"},
+		{"401", OAuthError, "HTTP 401"},
+		{"token expired", OAuthExpired, "token expired"},
+		{"session expired", OAuthExpired, "session expired"},
+		{"please log in", OAuthExpired, "login required"},
+		{"needs reauth", OAuthExpired, "needs reauthentication"},
+		{"refresh token", OAuthExpired, "refresh token issue"},
+	}
+
+	for _, p := range authErrorPatterns {
+		if strings.Contains(outputLower, p.pattern) {
+			return p.status, p.message
+		}
+	}
+
+	// If we see normal activity indicators, OAuth is likely valid
+	validIndicators := []string{
+		"thinking", "working", "reading", "writing",
+		"searching", "executing", "analyzing",
+	}
+	for _, ind := range validIndicators {
+		if strings.Contains(outputLower, ind) {
+			return OAuthValid, ""
+		}
+	}
+
+	return OAuthUnknown, ""
+}
+
+// detectRateLimitStatusFromOutput detects rate limit status from output
+func detectRateLimitStatusFromOutput(outputLower string) (RateLimitStatus, int) {
+	rateLimitPatterns := []string{
+		"rate limit", "ratelimit", "rate-limit",
+		"429", "too many requests", "quota exceeded",
+		"try again", "retry after", "backoff",
+	}
+
+	count := 0
+	for _, p := range rateLimitPatterns {
+		if strings.Contains(outputLower, p) {
+			count++
+		}
+	}
+
+	if count >= 3 {
+		return RateLimitLimited, count
+	}
+	if count >= 1 {
+		return RateLimitWarning, count
+	}
+	return RateLimitOK, 0
+}
+
+// countErrorsInOutput counts error patterns in output
+func countErrorsInOutput(outputLower string) int {
+	errorPatterns := []string{
+		"error", "failed", "exception", "panic",
+		"timeout", "connection refused",
+	}
+
+	count := 0
+	for _, p := range errorPatterns {
+		if strings.Contains(outputLower, p) {
+			count++
+		}
+	}
+	return count
+}
+
+// PrintHealthOAuth outputs per-agent OAuth and rate limit status for a session.
+// This is a thin wrapper around GetHealthOAuth() for CLI output.
+func PrintHealthOAuth(session string) error {
+	output, err := GetHealthOAuth(session)
+	if err != nil {
+		return err
+	}
+	return encodeJSON(output)
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,295 @@
+package panels
+
+import (
+	"fmt"
+	"strings"
+	"time"
+
+	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
+	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
+
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/robot"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/tui/layout"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/tui/theme"
+)
+
+// rateLimitConfig returns the configuration for the rate limit panel
+func rateLimitConfig() PanelConfig {
+	return PanelConfig{
+		ID:              "ratelimit",
+		Title:           "OAuth/Rate Status",
+		Priority:        PriorityHigh,
+		RefreshInterval: 5 * time.Second,
+		MinWidth:        30,
+		MinHeight:       6,
+		Collapsible:     true,
+	}
+}
+
+// RateLimitPanel displays OAuth and rate limit status per agent
+type RateLimitPanel struct {
+	PanelBase
+	agents []robot.AgentOAuthHealth
+	err    error
+}
+
+// NewRateLimitPanel creates a new rate limit status panel
+func NewRateLimitPanel() *RateLimitPanel {
+	return &RateLimitPanel{
+		PanelBase: NewPanelBase(rateLimitConfig()),
+	}
+}
+
+// SetData updates the panel with new OAuth/rate limit data
+func (m *RateLimitPanel) SetData(agents []robot.AgentOAuthHealth, err error) {
+	m.agents = agents
+	m.err = err
+}
+
+// HasError returns true if there's an active error
+func (m *RateLimitPanel) HasError() bool {
+	return m.err != nil
+}
+
+// Init implements tea.Model
+func (m *RateLimitPanel) Init() tea.Cmd {
+	return nil
+}
+
+// Update implements tea.Model
+func (m *RateLimitPanel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
+	return m, nil
+}
+
+// View implements tea.Model
+func (m *RateLimitPanel) View() string {
+	t := theme.Current()
+	w, h := m.Width(), m.Height()
+
+	if w <= 0 {
+		return ""
+	}
+
+	borderColor := t.Surface1
+	bgColor := t.Base
+	if m.IsFocused() {
+		borderColor = t.Pink
+		bgColor = t.Surface0
+	}
+
+	boxStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+		Background(bgColor).
+		Width(w).
+		Height(h)
+
+	// Build header with error badge if needed
+	title := m.Config().Title
+	if m.err != nil {
+		errorBadge := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+			Background(t.Red).
+			Foreground(t.Base).
+			Bold(true).
+			Padding(0, 1).
+			Render("!")
+		title = title + " " + errorBadge
+	}
+
+	header := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+		Bold(true).
+		Foreground(t.Text).
+		BorderStyle(lipgloss.NormalBorder()).
+		BorderBottom(true).
+		BorderForeground(borderColor).
+		Width(w).
+		Padding(0, 1).
+		Render(title)
+
+	var content strings.Builder
+	content.WriteString(header + "\n")
+
+	// Show error if present
+	if m.err != nil {
+		errorStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+			Foreground(t.Red).
+			Italic(true).
+			Padding(0, 1)
+		errMsg := layout.TruncateWidthDefault(m.err.Error(), w-6)
+		content.WriteString(errorStyle.Render("! "+errMsg) + "\n")
+		return boxStyle.Render(FitToHeight(content.String(), h))
+	}
+
+	if len(m.agents) == 0 {
+		content.WriteString("\n")
+		noAgentsStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+			Foreground(t.Subtext).
+			Italic(true).
+			Padding(0, 1)
+		content.WriteString(noAgentsStyle.Render("No agents to display") + "\n")
+		return boxStyle.Render(FitToHeight(content.String(), h))
+	}
+
+	// Calculate available lines for agent rows
+	availableLines := h - 2 // header + spacing
+	if availableLines < 1 {
+		availableLines = 1
+	}
+
+	// Render each agent row
+	for i, agent := range m.agents {
+		if i >= availableLines {
+			break
+		}
+
+		line := m.formatAgentRow(agent, w, t)
+		content.WriteString(line + "\n")
+	}
+
+	// Show overflow indicator if needed
+	if len(m.agents) > availableLines {
+		moreStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+			Foreground(t.Subtext).
+			Italic(true).
+			Padding(0, 1)
+		content.WriteString(moreStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("... +%d more", len(m.agents)-availableLines)) + "\n")
+	}
+
+	return boxStyle.Render(FitToHeight(content.String(), h))
+}
+
+// formatAgentRow formats a single agent's status line
+func (m *RateLimitPanel) formatAgentRow(agent robot.AgentOAuthHealth, width int, t theme.Theme) string {
+	// Format: "cc-1: OAuth=✓ Rate=OK  Last=2m ago"
+	var sb strings.Builder
+
+	// Agent identifier
+	agentID := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%d", m.shortAgentType(agent.AgentType), agent.Pane)
+	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  %s: ", agentID))
+
+	// OAuth status with color
+	oauthStyle := m.getOAuthStyle(agent.OAuthStatus, t)
+	oauthIcon := m.getOAuthIcon(agent.OAuthStatus)
+	sb.WriteString(oauthStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("OAuth=%s", oauthIcon)))
+	sb.WriteString(" ")
+
+	// Rate limit status with color
+	rateStyle := m.getRateLimitStyle(agent.RateLimitStatus, t)
+	rateIcon := m.getRateLimitIcon(agent.RateLimitStatus)
+	sb.WriteString(rateStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("Rate=%s", rateIcon)))
+	sb.WriteString(" ")
+
+	// Last activity
+	lastActivity := m.formatLastActivity(agent.LastActivitySec)
+	activityStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Subtext)
+	sb.WriteString(activityStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("Last=%s", lastActivity)))
+
+	// Cooldown remaining if applicable
+	if agent.CooldownRemaining > 0 {
+		cooldownStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Yellow)
+		sb.WriteString(cooldownStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" (%ds)", agent.CooldownRemaining)))
+	}
+
+	// Rate limit count if any
+	if agent.RateLimitCount > 0 && agent.RateLimitStatus != robot.RateLimitOK {
+		countStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Yellow)
+		sb.WriteString(countStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" (%d limits)", agent.RateLimitCount)))
+	}
+
+	// Truncate to width
+	line := sb.String()
+	if len(line) > width-2 {
+		line = line[:width-5] + "..."
+	}
+
+	return line
+}
+
+// shortAgentType returns a short agent type identifier
+func (m *RateLimitPanel) shortAgentType(agentType string) string {
+	switch agentType {
+	case "claude":
+		return "cc"
+	case "codex":
+		return "cod"
+	case "gemini":
+		return "gmi"
+	default:
+		return agentType[:min(3, len(agentType))]
+	}
+}
+
+// getOAuthStyle returns the style for OAuth status
+func (m *RateLimitPanel) getOAuthStyle(status robot.OAuthStatus, t theme.Theme) lipgloss.Style {
+	switch status {
+	case robot.OAuthValid:
+		return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Green)
+	case robot.OAuthExpired:
+		return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Yellow)
+	case robot.OAuthError:
+		return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Red)
+	default:
+		return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Subtext)
+	}
+}
+
+// getOAuthIcon returns the icon for OAuth status
+func (m *RateLimitPanel) getOAuthIcon(status robot.OAuthStatus) string {
+	switch status {
+	case robot.OAuthValid:
+		return "ok"
+	case robot.OAuthExpired:
+		return "exp"
+	case robot.OAuthError:
+		return "err"
+	default:
+		return "?"
+	}
+}
+
+// getRateLimitStyle returns the style for rate limit status
+func (m *RateLimitPanel) getRateLimitStyle(status robot.RateLimitStatus, t theme.Theme) lipgloss.Style {
+	switch status {
+	case robot.RateLimitOK:
+		return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Green)
+	case robot.RateLimitWarning:
+		return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Yellow)
+	case robot.RateLimitLimited:
+		return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Red)
+	default:
+		return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Subtext)
+	}
+}
+
+// getRateLimitIcon returns the icon for rate limit status
+func (m *RateLimitPanel) getRateLimitIcon(status robot.RateLimitStatus) string {
+	switch status {
+	case robot.RateLimitOK:
+		return "OK"
+	case robot.RateLimitWarning:
+		return "WARN"
+	case robot.RateLimitLimited:
+		return "LIM"
+	default:
+		return "?"
+	}
+}
+
+// formatLastActivity formats the last activity time
+func (m *RateLimitPanel) formatLastActivity(sec int) string {
+	if sec < 0 {
+		return "?"
+	}
+	if sec < 60 {
+		return fmt.Sprintf("%ds", sec)
+	}
+	if sec < 3600 {
+		return fmt.Sprintf("%dm", sec/60)
+	}
+	return fmt.Sprintf("%dh", sec/3600)
+}
+
+func min(a, b int) int {
+	if a < b {
+		return a
+	}
+	return b
+}
